lesson_manifest.petrov_stage: Build the first stable pronoun matrix and automate yes/no etiquette lines.
lesson_manifest.mission: Greet someone, introduce yourself, and give one short positive or negative statement.

lesson_manifest.petrov_stage: Open the matrix into question words and personal identification.
lesson_manifest.mission: Ask and answer a basic identity exchange with name, city, and place.

lesson_manifest.petrov_stage: Add high-frequency verbs without breaking the original pronoun frame.
lesson_manifest.mission: Ask what someone wants to eat or drink and answer with one clear preference.

lesson_manifest.petrov_stage: Move the same sentence logic into time, dates, and calendar talk.
lesson_manifest.mission: Say a date, weekday, and birthday-style calendar line without reading.

lesson_manifest.petrov_stage: Stretch the language into larger numbers, direction, and destination statements.
lesson_manifest.mission: Say where you want to go and combine a larger number with a place or direction line.

lesson_manifest.petrov_stage: Make time-of-day and knowing-language statements automatic.
lesson_manifest.mission: Say when something happens, whether you know it, and what language you can or cannot speak.

lesson_manifest.petrov_stage: Increase flexibility with how / can / still / already style questions.
lesson_manifest.mission: Ask how someone is, whether they can do something, and restate the line in another time frame.

lesson_manifest.petrov_stage: Turn the room outward into meeting people, age, and quantity dialogue.
lesson_manifest.mission: Meet someone politely, ask age or quantity questions, and answer without freezing.

lesson_manifest.petrov_stage: Stabilize demonstratives, classifiers, and comparison inside everyday talk.
lesson_manifest.mission: Use this/that with a classifier and give one short comparison.

lesson_manifest.petrov_stage: Push noun + color + classifier handling into shopping dialogue.
lesson_manifest.mission: Ask for clothing or shoes by color, item type, and quantity.

lesson_manifest.petrov_stage: Move the learner into restaurant choice and preference exchanges.
lesson_manifest.mission: Order a food or drink item and state one preference between two choices.

lesson_manifest.petrov_stage: Make service language and invitation dialogue polite and fast.
lesson_manifest.mission: Call the waiter, order politely, and ask for the menu or bill.

lesson_manifest.petrov_stage: Use dates, money, and experience talk without losing sentence control.
lesson_manifest.mission: State a big number, a date, and one have-you-ever-done-it line.

lesson_manifest.petrov_stage: Handle weather, temperature, and travel geography in live questions.
lesson_manifest.mission: Describe the weather or temperature and ask or answer one travel-direction question.

lesson_manifest.petrov_stage: Rework earlier material through polite address, etiquette, and family.
lesson_manifest.mission: Run a polite greeting or apology exchange, then add one family or frequency sentence.

lesson_manifest.petrov_stage: Collapse the whole course into biography, follow-up questions, and closing dialogue.
lesson_manifest.mission: Give a short biography, ask one follow-up question, and close with a social formula or toast.
